title: रङ्गनाथाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री रङ्गनाथाष्टकम् ॥

आनन्दरूपमजमद्वयमर्थशून्यं
सच्चित्सुखस्वरसविग्रहविश्वमूर्तिम्।
आनन्दकन्दमभिगम्य मुदा जनानां
रङ्गेशमाश्रयत मे मनसा सदैव॥ १॥

जगद्गुरुं जगदीशं जगत्पतिं
जगन्निवासमनन्तमजं विभुम्।
जगद्विमोहनमानन्दविग्रहं
भजामि रङ्गपतिं शरणं प्रपद्ये॥ २॥

आधिव्याधिविमर्दनं मुनिमनः-
प्रेमात्मसिन्धुं परं
शेषाद्रिप्रियमन्तरात्मनिलयं
श्रीरङ्गराजं भजे।
कावेरीतटमन्दिरे विलसतं
भक्तानुकम्पापरं
नारायणमनन्तमद्भुतमहा-
मूर्तिं सदा चिन्तये॥ ३॥

लक्ष्मीपतिं कमलनाभमजं मुकुन्दं
गोविन्दमच्युतमनन्तमजं पुराणम्।
विष्णुं विरिञ्चिनुतपादसरोजयुग्मं
श्रीरङ्गनाथमनिशं मनसा स्मरामि॥ ४॥

सहस्रशीर्षणमनन्तमनादिदेवं
गम्भीरनाभिकमलोद्भवब्रह्मगेहम्।
श्रीवत्सवक्षसमुदारविहारदक्षं
श्रीरङ्गराजमहमादरतो नमामि॥ ५॥

कौस्तुभप्रभामण्डलमण्डितं
वैजयन्तीवनमालाविराजितम्।
शङ्खचक्रगदापद्मविभूषितं
श्रीरङ्गनाथमहमाश्रयतां प्रपद्ये॥ ६॥

शेषपर्यङ्कमुपाश्रित्य शयानं
क्षीराब्धिमध्ये विलसन्तमनन्तभोगम्।
पद्मोद्भवस्तुतपदाम्बुजमद्भुताङ्गं
श्रीरङ्गनाथमभिवन्द्य सुखं लभामि॥ ७॥

संसारसागरमनन्तमपारगम्भी-
रं दुःखजालपरिवेष्टितमेव नित्यम्।
उद्धर्तुमेव शरणागतमीश्वरं त्वां
श्रीरङ्गनाथ कृपया परिपालय त्वम्॥ ८॥

रङ्गनाथाष्टकमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेत् भक्तिसंयुतः।
विष्णुलोकमवाप्नोति रङ्गनाथप्रसादतः॥

॥ इति श्री रङ्गनाथाष्टकम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ranganatha Ashtakam ||

Ananda-rupam-ajam-advayam-artha-shunyam
Sach-chit-sukha-svarasa-vigraha-vishva-murtim.
Ananda-kandam-abhigamya muda jananam
Rangesham-ashrayata me manasa sadaiva. || 1 ||

Jagad-gurum jagad-isham jagat-patim
Jagan-nivasam-anantam-ajam vibhum.
Jagad-vimohanam-ananda-vigraham
Bhajami Ranga-patim sharanam prapadye. || 2 ||

Adhi-vyadhi-vimardanam muni-manah-
prematma-sindhum param
Sheshadri-priyam-antaratma-nilayam
Shri Ranga-rajam bhaje.
Kaveri-tata-mandire vilasatam
bhakta-anukampa-param
Narayanam-anantam-adbhuta-maha-
murtim sada chintaye. || 3 ||

Lakshmi-patim Kamala-nabham-ajam Mukundam
Govindam-Achyutam-anantam-ajam puranam.
Vishnum Virinchi-nuta-pada-saroja-yugmam
Shri Ranganatham-anisham manasa smarami. || 4 ||

Sahasra-shirshanam-anantam-anadi-devam
Gambhira-nabhi-kamala-udbhava-Brahma-geham.
Shrivatsa-vaksasam-udara-vihara-daksham
Shri Ranga-rajam-aham-adarato namami. || 5 ||

Kaustubha-prabha-mandala-manditam
Vaijayanti-vanamala-virajitam.
Shankha-chakra-gada-padma-vibhushitam
Shri Ranganatham-aham-ashrayatam prapadye. || 6 ||

Shesha-paryankam-upashritya shayanam
Kshirabdhi-madhye vilasantam-ananta-bhogam.
Padmodbhava-stuta-padambujam-adbhutangam
Shri Ranganatham-abhivandya sukham labhami. || 7 ||

Samsara-sagaram-anantam-apara-gambhi-
ram duhkha-jala-pariveshtitam-eva nityam.
Uddhartum-eva sharanagartam-ishvaram tvam
Shri Ranganatha kripaya paripalaya tvam. || 8 ||

Ranganatha-ashtakam-idam punyam yah pathet bhakti-samyutah.
Vishnu-lokam-avapnoti Ranganatha-prasadatah.

|| Iti Shri Ranganatha Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
आनन्द स्वरूप, अज, अद्वय, सच्चिदानन्द विग्रह, विश्वमूर्ति, आनन्द के मूल रङ्गेश को मैं सदा मन से आश्रय करता हूँ॥ १॥

जगद्गुरु, जगदीश, जगत्पति, जगन्निवास, अनन्त, अज, विभु, जगत को मोहित करने वाले, आनन्द विग्रह रङ्गपति की मैं शरण लेता हूँ॥ २॥

आधि-व्याधि का विमर्दन करने वाले, मुनियों के मन रूपी प्रेम सिन्धु, कावेरी तट के मन्दिर में विलसित, भक्तों पर अनुकम्पा करने वाले, अनन्त, अद्भुत महामूर्ति नारायण श्री रंगराज को मैं भजता हूँ॥ ३॥

लक्ष्मीपति, कमलनाभ, अज, मुकुन्द, गोविन्द, अच्युत, अनन्त, पुराण, ब्रह्मा द्वारा चरण कमल वन्दित विष्णु श्री रंगनाथ को मैं निरन्तर स्मरण करता हूँ॥ ४॥

सहस्र शीर्षों वाले, अनन्त, अनादि देव, गम्भीर नाभि कमल से ब्रह्मा को उत्पन्न करने वाले, श्रीवत्स वक्ष वाले श्री रंगराज को मैं आदरपूर्वक नमन करता हूँ॥ ५॥

कौस्तुभ प्रभा से मण्डित, वैजयन्ती वनमाला से विराजित, शंख-चक्र-गदा-पद्म से विभूषित श्री रंगनाथ की शरण लेता हूँ॥ ६॥

शेष शय्या पर शयन करते हुए, क्षीरसागर में विलसित, अनन्त भोगशाली, ब्रह्मा द्वारा स्तुत चरण कमल वाले, अद्भुत अंगों वाले श्री रंगनाथ को प्रणाम कर मैं सुख प्राप्त करता हूँ॥ ७॥

इस रंगनाथ अष्टक को भक्तिपूर्वक पढ़ने वाला रंगनाथ की कृपा से विष्णुलोक को प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Invokes the blessing of the divine grace of lord ranganatha
Traditionally recited for removal of physical and mental ailments (adhi-vyadhi)
Invokes liberation and entry to vishnuloka
Purifies the mind and grants spiritual bliss
Protects devotees who surrender to the Lord
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires and brings peace
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Vishnu, Ranganatha Ashtakam (रङ्गनाथाष्टकम्) stands as an eight-verse devotional hymn. Rooted in Adi Shankaracharya / Traditional Vaishnavite Literature, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow the divine grace of Lord Ranganatha and remove physical and mental ailments (adhi-vyadhi). It is also said to fulfill desires and bring peace. This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Vaikuntha Ekadashi and Ekadashi for enhanced blessings. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Ranganatha Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ranganatha Ashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Eight verses glorifying Lord Ranganatha of Srirangam.

Sri Ranganatha Swamy Temple Srirangam Tamil Nadu

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ranganatha Ashtakam and which Vishnu temple does it celebrate?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ranganatha Ashtakam is an eight-verse Sanskrit hymn celebrating Lord Ranganatha — the reclining form of Vishnu enshrined at the Sri Ranganatha Swamy Temple in Srirangam, Tamil Nadu, on an island formed by the Kaveri river. The hymn opens with Ananda-rupam-ajam-advayam (the form of bliss, unborn, non-dual) and explicitly references the Kaveri-tata-mandire (temple on the bank of Kaveri) in verse 3, identifying Ranganatha as the principal deity worshipped there. Srirangam is the first and foremost of the 108 Divya Desams sacred to Sri Vaishnavism and the largest functioning Hindu temple complex in the world. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya stotra collections; Srirangam temple guides.)

seoFaqs.question: Was the Ranganatha Ashtakam composed by Adi Shankaracharya?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ranganatha Ashtakam is traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya (8th century CE) and is included in the Shankara stotra collections published by various mathas. While modern scholarship cannot definitively prove Shankara's authorship of every stotra attributed to him (many bhakti stotras attributed to Shankara are likely later devotional compositions in his style), the Ranganatha Ashtakam is included in the traditional Shankara devotional corpus alongside the Bhaja Govindam, Vishnu Shatpadi and others. The hymn's elevated philosophical tone (ananda-rupam, advayam, sach-chit-sukha) and Vedantic vocabulary are consistent with Shankara's style of devotional composition. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya Stotra Mala; traditional matha collections.)

seoFaqs.question: What is special about the Sri Ranganatha Swamy Temple in Srirangam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sri Ranganatha Swamy Temple in Srirangam is one of the most significant Vaishnava temples in India for several reasons. It is the largest functioning Hindu temple complex in the world, covering approximately 156 acres with seven concentric prakara walls and 21 gopurams (the Rajagopuram is one of Asia's tallest). It is the foremost of the 108 Divya Desams glorified by the Alvar saints. It was the home of the great Sri Vaishnava acharyas Nathamuni, Yamunacharya and Ramanujacharya. The Vaikuntha Ekadashi festival sees lakhs of devotees pass through the symbolic Vaikuntha Dvara (Sorga Vasal). The deity is in the Bhujanga-shayana posture reclining on Adisesha facing south, accompanied by his consort Sri Ranganayaki Thayar. (Sources: Wikipedia Ranganathaswamy Temple; UNESCO documentation.)

seoFaqs.question: What iconography of Lord Ranganatha is described in the hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses 5-7 describe the classical iconography. Verse 5: Sahasra-shirshanam (thousand-headed), Anantam (infinite), Anadi-devam (beginningless deity), with Brahma born from his deep navel-lotus, with Shrivatsa on his chest. Verse 6: Adorned with the Kaustubha gem's halo, the Vaijayanti vana-mala (forest garland), and decorated with the conch (Shankha), discus (Chakra), mace (Gada) and lotus (Padma). Verse 7: Reclining on the serpent-couch (Shesha-paryanka), in the midst of the cosmic milk-ocean (Kshirabdhi-madhye), with the lotus-born Brahma praising his lotus-feet. This is the cosmic Ranganatha simultaneously enshrined as the deity at Srirangam. (Sources: text verses 5-7; Srirangam temple iconography.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Ranganatha and the Sri Vaishnava tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Ranganatha is the principal deity of the Sri Vaishnava sampradaya — the major South Indian Vishnu-bhakti tradition systematised by Sri Ramanujacharya (1017-1137 CE). Ramanuja spent the bulk of his life at Srirangam serving Ranganatha, composed his Sri Bhashya commentary on the Brahma Sutras there, and is enshrined within the temple complex (his preserved body in lotus posture in the Ramanuja Sannidhi). The 12 Alvar saints sang of Ranganatha in their Naalayira Divya Prabandham. Andal — the only female Alvar — composed the Tiruppavai pining for Ranganatha as her bridegroom and ultimately merged into him at Srirangam. The Ranganatha Ashtakam fits naturally into this rich Sri Vaishnava devotional tradition. (Sources: Sri Vaishnava history; Ramanujacharya biographies; Naalayira Divya Prabandham.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ranganatha Ashtakam phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti declares Ranganatha-ashtakam idam punyam yah pathet bhakti-samyutah, Vishnu-lokam-avapnoti Ranganatha-prasadatah — one who recites this auspicious Ranganatha Ashtakam united with devotion attains Vishnu-loka by the grace of Ranganatha. Verses within the body of the text promise the destruction of mental and physical ailments (adhi-vyadhi-vimardanam in verse 3), liberation from the ocean of samsara filled with sorrow (verse 8), and the bliss of taking refuge (sukham labhami in verse 7). The hymn is therefore both a daily devotional prayer and an ultimate moksha-stuti, particularly powerful when recited at Srirangam itself or during Vaikuntha Ekadashi. (Sources: text phalashruti; traditional Sri Vaishnava commentaries.)
